purpose: Split research candidates into near-term tactical setups and long-term macro/portfolio-balance candidates.
operatingRule: Candidates are research objects, not buy instructions. Promotion requires source evidence, add zone, invalidation, portfolio role, and risk budget.

tickerOfMoment.name: Hims & Hers
tickerOfMoment.thesis: Experimental consumer-health platform candidate: potentially interesting if direct-to-consumer healthcare keeps taking share and category expansion is disciplined.
confirmBeforeAdd: Retention and margin quality support growth
Regulatory/product concentration risk is controlled
Entry is based on valuation reset not social momentum
tickerOfMoment.portfolioFit: Candidate may diversify portfolio factor exposure away from high-beta AI/liquidity concentration.

tickerOfMoment.name: GE Vernova
tickerOfMoment.thesis: A creative infrastructure candidate for the electricity scarcity layer: grid hardware, gas turbines, and electrification may be a more durable AI-adjacent bottleneck than another software/platform add.
confirmBeforeAdd: Orders/backlog support multi-year grid demand
Margin execution is improving rather than just narrative-driven
Entry price is not stretched versus industrial peers
tickerOfMoment.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.

tickerOfMoment.name: Reddit
tickerOfMoment.thesis: Experimental attention/data asset: interesting if human conversation data becomes a scarce AI-training/licensing input and ad monetization keeps improving.
confirmBeforeAdd: Revenue growth and margin path are improving
Data licensing is durable not one-off
User/community risk is priced in
tickerOfMoment.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.

tickerOfMoment.name: Broadcom
tickerOfMoment.thesis: Picks-and-shovels exposure to AI networking and custom ASIC demand with a more diversified enterprise/software profile than pure GPU exposure.
confirmBeforeAdd: AI semiconductor growth offsets cyclicality
Debt/software integration risk remains controlled
Price action is not purely multiple expansion
tickerOfMoment.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.

tickerOfMoment.name: Vertiv
tickerOfMoment.thesis: Infrastructure-side exposure to AI/data-center buildout: power, cooling, and uptime constraints rather than model/platform winners.
confirmBeforeAdd: Backlog/order growth supports the AI infrastructure thesis
Margins remain resilient as capacity scales
Entry price compensates for crowded theme risk
tickerOfMoment.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.

tickerOfMoment.name: NVIDIA
tickerOfMoment.thesis: Direct exposure to AI accelerator demand; useful benchmark for whether the portfolio should own the center of the AI capex stack instead of only cloud/platform beneficiaries.
confirmBeforeAdd: Forward revenue/gross-margin durability remains intact
Valuation reset or earnings growth justifies multiple
Customer concentration and export-control risks are understood
tickerOfMoment.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.

longTermMacroFit.name: Eaton
longTermMacroFit.thesis: A less flashy but potentially higher-quality way to express the grid/data-center power constraint without owning only the hottest AI names.
confirmBeforeAdd: Data-center/electrical segment growth remains durable
Valuation reset offers margin of safety
Balance sheet and cash conversion remain strong
longTermMacroFit.portfolioFit: Candidate may diversify portfolio factor exposure away from high-beta AI/liquidity concentration.

longTermMacroFit.name: Alphabet
longTermMacroFit.thesis: Potential quality compounder if search/cloud/AI investment remains durable and valuation offers a cleaner margin of safety than hotter AI infrastructure names.
confirmBeforeAdd: Cloud and AI monetization progress is visible
Regulatory/search risks are priced in
Relative valuation is attractive versus existing platform holdings
longTermMacroFit.portfolioFit: Candidate may diversify portfolio factor exposure away from high-beta AI/liquidity concentration.

longTermMacroFit.name: Quanta Services
longTermMacroFit.thesis: A picks-and-shovels candidate for the physical grid buildout needed by electrification, data centers, and energy transition.
confirmBeforeAdd: Backlog and utility capex visibility remain strong
Labor/input cost risk is controlled
Price enters prepared add zone
longTermMacroFit.portfolioFit: Candidate may diversify portfolio factor exposure away from high-beta AI/liquidity concentration.

longTermMacroFit.name: Nextracker
longTermMacroFit.thesis: More experimental energy-infrastructure idea: not just AI power demand, but grid-edge hardware and utility-scale solar deployment efficiency.
confirmBeforeAdd: Bookings/backlog support growth
Margins are not peaking cyclically
Policy/tariff risks are understood
longTermMacroFit.portfolioFit: Candidate may diversify portfolio factor exposure away from high-beta AI/liquidity concentration.

longTermMacroFit.name: TransMedics
longTermMacroFit.thesis: A genuinely different growth infrastructure idea: organ transplant logistics and preservation network effects, far from current AI/platform concentration.
confirmBeforeAdd: Procedure growth and reimbursement remain supportive
Margins scale with network utilization
Competition and execution risks are understood
longTermMacroFit.portfolioFit: Candidate may diversify portfolio factor exposure away from high-beta AI/liquidity concentration.

longTermMacroFit.name: Palantir
longTermMacroFit.thesis: A controversial but interesting candidate: if AI shifts from model infrastructure to operational deployment, PLTR may be a pure workflow/ontology beneficiary.
confirmBeforeAdd: Revenue growth and margin quality justify valuation
Commercial adoption is not hype-only
Entry follows valuation reset or major evidence upgrade
longTermMacroFit.portfolioFit: Candidate may diversify portfolio factor exposure away from high-beta AI/liquidity concentration.

longTermMacroFit.name: GE Vernova
longTermMacroFit.thesis: A creative infrastructure candidate for the electricity scarcity layer: grid hardware, gas turbines, and electrification may be a more durable AI-adjacent bottleneck than another software/platform add.
confirmBeforeAdd: Orders/backlog support multi-year grid demand
Margin execution is improving rather than just narrative-driven
Entry price is not stretched versus industrial peers
longTermMacroFit.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.

longTermMacroFit.thesis: Infrastructure-side exposure to AI/data-center buildout: power, cooling, and uptime constraints rather than model/platform winners.
confirmBeforeAdd: Backlog/order growth supports the AI infrastructure thesis
Margins remain resilient as capacity scales
Entry price compensates for crowded theme risk
longTermMacroFit.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.

allCandidates.name: Cameco
allCandidates.thesis: A creative non-AI software expression of the power scarcity thesis: nuclear fuel exposure if electricity demand and energy security remain structurally important.
confirmBeforeAdd: Uranium contracting/pricing remains supportive
Position size respects commodity cyclicality
Entry is not late-cycle momentum chasing
allCandidates.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.

allCandidates.name: iShares Bitcoin Trust
allCandidates.thesis: A cleaner vehicle to compare against levered/path-dependent crypto exposure; may reduce decay risk while preserving directional Bitcoin beta.
confirmBeforeAdd: Explicit risk budget for crypto beta
Liquidity regime supports risk assets
Expense, tracking, and drawdown profile beat levered alternatives
allCandidates.portfolioFit: Candidate may diversify portfolio factor exposure away from high-beta AI/liquidity concentration.

allCandidates.name: Rocket Lab
allCandidates.thesis: A creative industrial-space candidate: asymmetric if launch cadence, defense/customer demand, and satellite systems compound into infrastructure status.
confirmBeforeAdd: Launch cadence/backlog improves
Gross margin path is credible
Risk budget treats it as speculative growth
allCandidates.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.

allCandidates.thesis: Experimental attention/data asset: interesting if human conversation data becomes a scarce AI-training/licensing input and ad monetization keeps improving.

allCandidates.name: Oklo
allCandidates.thesis: Highly experimental asymmetric optionality on advanced nuclear power. This belongs in the creative lab, not the core portfolio, unless evidence improves dramatically.
confirmBeforeAdd: Licensing/regulatory milestones are concrete
Cash runway and dilution risk are understood
Position is capped as venture-style optionality
allCandidates.portfolioFit: Candidate does not obviously worsen a dominant current exposure.
